body:
The "Year of Return" campaign has painted a picture of Ghana as a perfect African paradise — a real-life Wakanda where diaspora can escape racism and find instant belonging.

Ghana is beautiful. Ghana is vibrant. Ghana is welcoming. Ghana is also a real country with real challenges. To arrive expecting perfection is to set yourself up for disappointment, and to disrespect the reality of life for millions of Ghanaians.

"Since the Year of Return, I cannot afford to go to my favourite clubs anymore. Service providers see diaspora paying in dollars and they have raised prices for everyone." — Effia Afful, Accra.
subtitle: 15 min · Audio
takeaways:
Gain deep insight into ghana is not wakanda — managing expectations
Align expectations with real-world context
title: Ghana is Not Wakanda — Managing Expectations

body:
This is the part most diaspora visitors never hear before they arrive. These are uncomfortable truths. But if you truly want to build bridges with locals, you must understand the tensions that exist.

In four segments — land disputes, economic segregation, condescension, and the right way — you will hear directly from Ghanaian voices, in their own words.
subtitle: 10 min · Video
takeaways:
Gain deep insight into the uncomfortable truths
Align expectations with real-world context
title: The Uncomfortable Truths

body:
You are about to embark on something profound — a return to the land of your ancestors. But this is not a simple homecoming. It is a journey of reconnection, and reconnection is complex, emotional, and sometimes uncomfortable.

Over the next six stages, you will learn not from Western experts, but from us — Ghanaians and Africans across the continent. You will hear our voices, our frustrations, our hopes, and our expectations.
subtitle: 10 min · Video
takeaways: Akwaaba, friend. Welcome. My name is Ama. I will be one of your guides on this journey.
title: Welcome — Your Journey Begins
